title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Italians in a particular historical period had a different cultural and political perspective on the Pope compared to other European populations. The conversation explores the unique relationship between Italian society and papal authority.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer explains why Italians in a particular historical period viewed the Pope differently than other European populations did. The discussion centers on the distinct cultural and political relationship between Italian society and papal authority. The takeaway: Italian proximity to the papacy shaped a perspective on the Pope that diverged from how the rest of Europe understood that authority.
